title: MAS to boost Singapore equities market with education, guardrails and a risk‑taking framework
body: MAS deputy chairman Chee Hong Tat announced increased resources for public and investor education, new guardrails and a more risk‑taking approach to revive Singapore’s equities market, while emphasizing transparency, shareholder engagement and stronger enforcement. He also responded to Workers’ Party proposals, noting that some value‑creation disclosures may become mandatory but stressing that not all measures should be prescribed.

title: SGX posts strongest half‑year results since 2000, driven by higher trading volumes and IPO activity
body: SGX reported a half‑year net profit of $342.7 million (up 0.8%) and revenue of $736.2 million (up 7.9%), with daily average securities trading value rising 20% to $1.51 billion—the highest in five years, and an interim dividend of 21.75 cents per share, up 20.8% year‑on‑year. The exchange attributed the performance to stronger multi‑asset trading, a robust IPO pipeline of more than 30 companies and the Equity Market Development Programme supported by the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

title: Yangzijiang Shipbuilding fell about 5.6% after a drop in Maersk earnings and job‑cut plans, pulling Singapore's Straits Times Index down 0.7%
body: Yangzijiang Shipbuilding’s shares slid 5.3% to 5.6% in early trading, erasing over S$700 million in market value, after Maersk reported lower earnings and announced plans to cut 1,000 jobs, with Maersk shares down 3.7% in Copenhagen. The broader market saw the STI down 0.7% and declines in DBS (‑0.7%), OCBC (‑0.9%) and Singtel (‑0.8%), while regional indices and US tech stocks also fell.

title: SGX posts modest profit rise, raises interim dividend and highlights strong IPO pipeline
body: SGX reported H1 FY2026 adjusted net profit of S$357.1 million (up 11.6%) with revenue of S$736.2 million and declared an interim dividend of S$0.11 per share payable on Feb 24. CEO Loh Boon Chye said trading broadened across sectors, institutional investors bought S$415 million of small‑ and mid‑cap stocks, the exchange raised nearly S$3 billion from IPOs and is preparing a Global Listing Board to attract more high‑growth companies.

title: Keppel shares jump as it proposes 13‑cent dividend while M1 stake sale to Simba Telecom faces regulatory delay
body: Keppel's shares rose over 6% after announcing a 13‑cent special dividend, but the expected $1.43 billion sale of its 83.9% stake in M1 to Simba Telecom is delayed pending regulatory approval, with cash proceeds of about $1 billion slated for recognition in the 2026 monetisation value. The company also highlighted the need to boost occupancy at its Keppel South Central office tower before any further divestments and outlined its early‑stage AI initiatives and competitive positioning against firms such as BlackRock and Global Infrastructure Partners.

title: Asian markets slide as AI spending worries hit Singapore’s STI and crypto prices tumble
body: The Straits Times Index fell 0.8% to 4,934.41 as investors reacted to Amazon’s $200 billion AI‑related spending plan and broader tech sell‑off, dragging down blue‑chip stocks such as Yangzijiang Shipbuilding (‑6.2%), DBS (‑0.6%), OCBC (‑1%) and Singtel (‑1.3%). Bitcoin slipped to around US$63,000, a 50% drop over three months, with Maybank analysts linking the crypto decline to rising AI costs and the nomination of hawkish Fed chair candidate Kevin Warsh, while regional indices showed mixed moves.

title: Singapore Exchange posts slight profit increase amid earnings‑season rally while Singtel, Keppel and Hongkong Land announce major deals and dividend upgrades
body: Singapore Exchange SGX reported a 0.8% rise in first‑half net profit to $342.7 million and a 7.9% revenue increase, declared a 21.75 cent interim dividend and reiterated 6‑8% medium‑term revenue growth despite a modest share dip. During the same week, Singtel teamed with KKR in a $6.6 billion acquisition of ST Telemedia Global Data Centres, Keppel posted a 39% jump in FY2025 profit to $1.1 billion and proposed a 47 cent total dividend, Hongkong Land launched an $8.2 billion Singapore Central Private Real Estate Fund, and parliament debated the effectiveness of the Equity Market Development Programme.

title: Boustead Singapore moves ahead with UI Boustead REIT listing and divests industrial assets
body: Boustead Singapore received an eligibility-to-list letter from the Singapore Exchange for UI Boustead REIT and entered definitive agreements to sell its interests in 15 industrial properties, generating about $258.7 million in proceeds, of which roughly $202.8 million will be used to subscribe for a 16.9 % stake in the REIT. Metro Holdings, through its subsidiary Metrobilt Construction, will sell its 26 % stake in Boustead Industrial Fund to the REIT for an estimated $116 million, while Boustead shares traded at $2.15, down 1.4 % on Feb 10.

title: SGX reports 66% YoY turnover rise in January as STI gains 5.6% and new listings boost activity
body: The Singapore Exchange posted a S$34.6 billion turnover in January, up 66% year‑on‑year, with securities daily average value rising 58% to S$1.6 billion and ETF assets reaching a record S$19.1 billion. The STI climbed 5.6% month‑on‑month, while new listings such as Concord New Energy Group, The Assembly Place and Toku, and fresh ETFs including UOBAM Ping An FTSE Asean Dividend Index ETF and CSOP CSAM CSI A500 Index ETF, contributed to heightened trading across equities, derivatives and commodities.

title: Mindarie Investment's cash offer for Alpha Integrated Real Estate Investment Trust AI-Reit becomes unconditional, closing date extended to Feb 24
body: Mindarie Investment's mandatory conditional cash offer for Alpha Integrated Real Estate Investment Trust AI-Reit was declared unconditional on Feb 9 after acceptances covering about 10% of units, raising Volare and its concert parties' holdings to roughly 51.2% of the REIT’s issued units. The closing date was extended to 5.30 pm on Feb 24, and UOB, the offeror’s financial adviser, said the REIT will stay listed with no material changes planned.

title: Singapore's STI breaks 5,000 points as banks rally and market reforms boost confidence
body: The Straits Times Index climbed above 5,000 on Feb 12, driven by gains in its three major banks—DBS up 0.5%, OCBC up 1.3% and UOB up 0.9%—and supported by MAS and SGX initiatives to enhance liquidity and attract listings. The rise follows a $5 billion equity market development programme, a new iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index, a dual‑listing highway with Nasdaq, and a reduced board‑lot size aimed at revitalising Singapore’s equities market.

title: OCBC downgrades Sheng Siong to hold, shares fall 4.2%
body: Sheng Siong’s shares dropped 4.2% to $2.73 after OCBC downgraded the stock to “hold,” citing a forward 12‑month P/E of 24.8 versus its 19.6 historical average. The analyst kept a defensive outlook, noted strong earnings visibility, and raised the fair‑value target to $2.89.
